content.body: Thermodynamics studies heat and its relationship to energy and work. The four laws of thermodynamics describe fundamental physical principles.

First Law: ΔU = Q - W
(Change in internal energy = Heat added - Work done by system)

Second Law: Heat flows naturally from hot to cold
Entropy of isolated system never decreases

Boyle's Law: PV = constant (constant T)
Charles' Law: V/T = constant (constant P)

content.title: First Law Example
content.body:
500 J of heat is added to a gas that does 200 J of work.
ΔU = Q - W = 500 - 200 = 300 J
The internal energy increases by 300 J

questions.question: The First Law of Thermodynamics states:
options: Energy cannot be created or destroyed
Heat always flows from cold to hot
questions.answer: Energy cannot be created or destroyed
questions.explanation: The First Law is conservation of energy: ΔU = Q - W

questions.question: If 300 J is added to a system that does 100 J of work, ΔU is:
Boyle's Law applies at constant:
questions.explanation: Boyle's Law: PV = constant, only at constant temperature (isothermal)

questions.question: What does entropy measure?
questions.explanation: Entropy is a measure of disorder in a system

questions.question: A gas is compressed at constant temperature. Its pressure:
questions.explanation: By Boyle's Law: if V decreases and T is constant, P must increase
